title: The Dead Village
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 2's final approach to Millhaven.
openLocation: Millhaven, just south of the Quel'Thalas borderlands.
transport: Mounted approach on Thalassian horses; on foot during the village sweep.
immediateObjective: Read Millhaven, find out what happened to its people, and decide whether the danger is really moving toward Quel'Thalas.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas perception only.
No grain confirmation or organized Cult proof in this chapter.
No thaw, trust, or easy rapport between Sylvanas and Jaina.
The old grain seller must read as useful local intelligence, not an exposed threat.

povKnowsAtOpen: The contamination is real, physical, and big enough to matter beyond one village.
Jaina's readings are useful even when her sense of the road is poor.
Millhaven is the first place on the south road where the wrongness knots tight.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: How it first got into the village.
How large the threat is beyond this village.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No assumption that the old trader is anything but a local source.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Millhaven is the first fully claimed village on this road.
The survivor girl's testimony plus the follow-up tracking pass confirm the dead marched north toward Quel'Thalas.
Neral rides north with the girl and warning, leaving only Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel to keep riding south.
The newer grain-seller mention enters the file as a weak unresolved clue.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: This is still border defense to read, report, and answer, not a human catastrophe Sylvanas has to own on its own terms.
pov.surfaceObjective: Read Millhaven cleanly enough to turn shock into a warning she can send home before the road worsens again.
pov.pressureSource: The village is not a rumor or a bad patrol report. It is a whole settlement gone wrong, with missing dead, one living witness, and a kind of wrongness Sylvanas cannot price without Jaina.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas keeps treating Jaina as a specialist she can consult when needed, not as half of the field read.
pov.chapterTest: Millhaven forces her to weigh tracks, corpses, silence, witness testimony, and magic together. None of it resolves cleanly if she cuts out Jaina's side.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep Jaina on a short leash as a useful prisoner, or start letting her read shape what the party does next.
pov.endStateShift: By chapter close Sylvanas still does not trust Jaina, but she stops treating the mage's read as optional color. It is part of the map now.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 4 opens with Sylvanas expecting Jaina to speak when the wrongness turns strange, even if authority still stays in Sylvanas's hands.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The only woman here who can tell how deep the wrongness goes and which parts of it matter.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Useful, but still a human mage who might see the magical half more clearly than the ground under their boots.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Jaina does not just name the wrongness. She helps separate what is loud from what matters, which keeps the field read from collapsing into blind horror.
throughPov.interactionRule: Sylvanas still withholds warmth. Jaina earns more room to speak, not comfort.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Custody ride under pressure. Useful prisoner, armed escort, no trust.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The village makes side-by-side reading unavoidable. By the end, they are building one picture out of two kinds of evidence.
team.namingAttitudeRule: No easy softening yet. If Sylvanas uses Jaina's name in her own head, it should land because the mage's read is doing real work, not because the mood warmed.
team.doNotOverstateRule: This is first proof of usefulness, not rapport.

entries.rule: Remains with the party and is the ranger Sylvanas trusts most on the ground.
Stays with the party into Chapter 4.

Present at chapter open. She stays through the cellar rescue and the follow-up tracking pass; once the girl's testimony and the ground read together show the risen dead are likely mustering toward Quel'Thalas, she is sent north with the warning dispatch and exits the traveling party this chapter.
Present at chapter open as the spare fourth rider from Chapter 2. She leaves north with Neral and the survivor so the southbound group reduces cleanly to the four named leads.
Found in a cellar this chapter. She does not travel with Sylvanas and Jaina afterward; Neral escorts her north on horseback once the warning dispatch leaves.
First Mal'Ganis disguise. Must read as useful, local, unremarkable, and not yet suspicious to the POV.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 4 opens after a two-day southbound move. By then the traveling party is Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel only.

scenes.title: Millhaven
scenes.sceneFunction: Show the first village the plague has truly taken, and make the missing dead a real problem.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The contamination is real and physical.
How it got here is still unclear.
scenes.arcPressure: Sylvanas can still treat Jaina's magic as consultative until Millhaven gives them a disaster spoor alone cannot solve.

enrichment.detail: Northern Lordaeron border villages should read as rough timber-and-thatch settlements on muddy agricultural roads, with Millhaven's intact crossroads normalcy making the silence worse rather than picturesque. Harvest-time livestock smell and household routine are the baseline the dead quiet erases.

beats.goal: Enter the village carefully and assess whether it is occupied, trapped, or abandoned.
beats.conflict: Millhaven is intact but acoustically dead; every normal village sound is missing.

outcome.summary: The party gains entry, but the absence itself is worse than visible damage.

newOnPageInformation: Millhaven was not burned, sacked, or visibly fought over.
The wrongness is recent enough that ordinary life still seems interrupted rather than erased.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Where the people are.
Whether anything inside is still living.
beats.transitionIntent: action continuation: Sylvanas answers the silence with a disciplined sweep instead of yielding the whole read to Jaina's magic.
beats.verifierNotes: Millhaven is already established from chapter2's closing approach; treat this as entry escalation, not a fresh village introduction.

beats.goal: Read the village for timeline and population fate.
beats.conflict: Every building says left in the middle of living while offering no bodies and no obvious kill site.

outcome.summary: The sweep produces evidence of staggered collapse but no explanation for the vanished population.

newOnPageInformation: The collapse took days, not a single sudden event.
Domestic life froze in place before emptying out.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the missing population fled, was taken, or rose.
beats.transitionIntent: action continuation: the empty houses push Jaina toward the most saturated interiors to see whether the contamination gives up any clearer pattern.

beats.goal: Let Jaina test whether the village gives up how this started.
beats.conflict: The whole place reads wrong: well, soil, root cellar, granary, trough.

outcome.summary: Jaina proves the whole village is soaked in it, but she still cannot say what first brought it in.

beats.valueShift: impossible absence -> the whole village is soaked, but the cause is still slippery
newOnPageInformation: The granary is the strongest single read, but Jaina refuses to mistake the loudest layer for the whole answer.
Sylvanas is starting to treat Jaina as the one kind of reader she does not have.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether it came through grain, water, soil, or some slower layering.
beats.transitionIntent: action continuation: Jaina's inconclusive read sends Sylvanas back outside to put ranger hands on the ground.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina's contamination-reading authority is established; this beat expands scope and usefulness rather than unveiling surprise competence.

beats.goal: Determine what happened to the bodies.
beats.conflict: The ground behind the village is too churned and partial to call cleanly; there are drag marks, broken crust, and trampled exits, but not yet a direction Sylvanas will stake a border warning on.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas confirms many of the dead left Millhaven on their feet, but the ground alone does not yet prove where they went.

beats.valueShift: the whole village is soaked, but the cause is still slippery -> too much danger, not enough proof
newOnPageInformation: A large number of bodies are missing from the village.
The dead did not simply collapse in place and stay there.
Sylvanas needs either a witness or a cleaner line of spoor before she commits a warning north.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the dead moved toward Quel'Thalas or elsewhere.
Who is directing them.
Where the rest of the villagers are.
beats.transitionIntent: planted question: spoor alone cannot carry the warning, so the next beat needs either a living witness or cleaner proof.

sceneLevelWarnings: Scene 1 runs four straight scene beats, all darkening the chapter before any sequel relief arrives. The pressure is usable, but the opening stays intentionally heavy.

scenes.title: The Survivor and the Proof
scenes.sceneFunction: Put a living child inside the disaster, then use her to turn guesswork into warning.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The ground alone has not yet proved where the dead went.
scenes.arcPressure: The witness only becomes actionable once Sylvanas lets testimony, magic, and ranger ground-read build one picture together.

enrichment.detail: Canonical plague spread in Lordaeron runs through tainted food supplies, with victims dying within days and rising feral soon after. Keep the girl's testimony on sickness, death, and night movement rather than letting anyone solve the delivery mechanism on-page yet.

beats.goal: Find any living witness left in Millhaven.
beats.conflict: They locate an alive but traumatized girl hidden in a cellar, dehydrated, terrified, and unreliable in sequence.

outcome.summary: They get a witness, but the testimony is fragmentary and emotionally broken.

beats.valueShift: too much danger, not enough proof -> living testimony at last
newOnPageInformation: At least one living person survived inside the village.
Neral is the first ranger the girl does not recoil from, which makes her the natural escort if the child has to be moved.
The girl confirms death, rising, and night movement rather than simple disease collapse.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Exact timeline.
Direction of the dead.
How it first got in.
beats.transitionIntent: dialogue hook: the survivor can speak, but only in broken pieces, so the next beat pulls on fragments rather than a clean timeline.

beats.goal: Pull usable intelligence from the survivor.
beats.conflict: The girl remembers in images, not order: sweetness on people's breath, neighbors getting sick, bodies rising, dead shuffling out toward the north road at night, adults telling children to stay hidden, and complaint about a newer grain man on the route.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gains confirmation that the dead rose and moved with direction, and hears one early oddity in who had been selling grain, but the child's memory still needs the ground to back it.

beats.valueShift: living testimony at last -> horror with direction implied
newOnPageInformation: Some risen dead came back for the sick who remained.
The girl remembers movement toward the north road rather than blind milling.
There was talk of a newer grain seller before the collapse.
The girl is too unstable to build a full timeline from alone.
Jaina is the one who can pull the few useful pieces out of the girl's broken story, which Sylvanas notices.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the northbound memory matches the physical trail cleanly enough to warn Quel'Thalas.
Whether the grain clue matters or is incidental.
beats.transitionIntent: planted question: the girl points north, but Sylvanas still needs the ground to agree before she spends riders on warning.
beats.verifierNotes: Keep the newer grain seller as a weak clue only; the delivery mechanism is still unresolved at this point.

beats.goal: Verify whether the girl's broken memory matches the ground outside Millhaven.
beats.conflict: The road cut north of the village is damaged by wagon ruts, scavengers, and too many overlapping exits, so the read has to be made from scraps instead of a clean trail.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas and Cyndia finally confirm a north-northeast mass movement toward Quel'Thalas, and the answer immediately widens the strategic emergency.

beats.valueShift: horror with direction implied -> the dead marched toward home
newOnPageInformation: The girl's testimony and the physical spoor now support each other.
At least sixty reanimated bodies moved north in rough formation.
No idea what prompted it.
Jaina's magical read helps strip ordinary road damage out of the spoor, which Sylvanas files as another kind of tracking whether she likes the comparison or not.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the dead are bunching up on their own or being pushed into line by living hands.
Whether the warning will arrive in time to matter.
beats.transitionIntent: action continuation: once the northbound movement is confirmed, the chapter has to turn proof into dispatch and separation.

scenes.title: The Grain Seller on the Road
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn proof into action, bring in the old trader as a useful local, send Neral north with the girl, and close on the smaller group riding south.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The girl and the ground together have confirmed the dead are moving north.
The child cannot stay with the investigation.
Neral is the obvious dispatch rider.
"New grain man" exists only as one witness fragment.
scenes.arcPressure: Sylvanas has to split her strength—warn home now and keep pushing south—while the first hidden hand on the road passes as ordinary help.

enrichment.detail: Mal'Ganis canonically oversees plague operations in Lordaeron and later passes as mundane mortal authority, so the trader works best as helpful, road-wise, and instantly forgettable rather than sinister. The farewell can snag on Sylvanas's ear without turning the encounter into an exposed reveal.

beats.emotion: Urgency with no time to indulge it.
beats.dilemma: Keep Neral for the road south, or lock the warning in her hands now and accept that the split will be real the moment the last road read is done.
beats.decision: Put the warning in Neral's hands, line the girl up with her escort, and let one last road contact sharpen the picture before the directions actually split.
beats.valueShift: the dead marched toward home -> warning assigned, split imminent
newOnPageInformation: Neral is the best rider to carry a warning north in a hurry.
Neral is unexpectedly good with frightened children, which makes the choice cleaner than a purely tactical handoff.
The unnamed fourth rider folds into Neral's northbound escort, which will leave the southbound group at the four named leads once the parting is complete.
Sylvanas is prioritizing the border threat even while continuing south.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the warning will arrive in time to matter.
beats.transitionIntent: action continuation: the warning is assigned, but one last road contact can sharpen the picture before the directions actually split.
beats.verifierModification: Shifted the dispatch from already-complete split to split-imminent so beat 9 can still keep Neral and the girl on-page without a state contradiction.

beats.goal: Gather route intelligence and see how far the outbreak has progressed beyond Millhaven.
beats.conflict: An old grain seller on the road north has exactly the kind of unforced local knowledge a field commander values, and finding Millhaven totally gone catches him visibly off guard instead of confirming anything he expected.

outcome.summary: He gives useful road talk, and his surprise makes it clear Millhaven shocked him too, but the party still has to trust a stranger's read of the country ahead.

beats.valueShift: warning assigned, split imminent -> the road picture gets clearer
newOnPageInformation: Settlements ahead are still trading and only lightly touched by sickness.
Millhaven is the first village on this road fully claimed by the plague.
The trader's empty wagon is heading north, and he clearly did not expect to find the village silent.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: That the trader is linked to the contamination itself.
beats.transitionIntent: sensory carry: the trader's dust, wagon, and practical road talk carry into the parting, where one odd farewell snags without taking over the scene.

beats.goal: Complete the parting cleanly and move on.
beats.conflict: The trader's farewell lands just oddly enough to earn Sylvanas a brief glance back, but the moment is too thin to matter more than the dead and the road south.

outcome.summary: The parting completes cleanly, though Sylvanas gives him one second look before letting the matter go.

beats.valueShift: the road picture gets clearer -> parting complete, eyes south again
newOnPageInformation: The survivor girl leaves the chapter here under Neral's escort, headed north on horseback.
Sylvanas gives the trader a brief look back before turning south again.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: The trader's real identity.
Whether the trader matters at all beyond this practical handoff.
beats.transitionIntent: intentional hard cut: Sylvanas lets the odd farewell drop and turns the chapter's focus back to the reduced southbound group.
beats.verifierNotes: Do not weight the trader's odd farewell like an exposed reveal; it should read as a passing snag, not a solved clue.

beats.dilemma: Turn back after warning the border, or keep following the stronger readings and the live-settlement trail.
beats.decision: Keep moving south toward the next still-living village.
beats.valueShift: parting complete, attention pulled south again -> committed pursuit
newOnPageInformation: Only Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel are riding south now.
They are committing to a multi-day push deeper into Lordaeron.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Grain as the thing carrying it.
The organization behind the plague.
beats.transitionIntent: intentional hard cut: chapter4 opens two days later, with the quartet still riding south and the next village still misreading what is happening to it.

departuresLocked: Neral is sent north with the warning dispatch and the survivor girl.
The unnamed escort ranger leaves north with Neral and the survivor girl.
The survivor girl exits the investigation party under Neral's escort.
factsLockedForChapter4: Millhaven is dead, and the survivor girl's testimony plus the follow-up tracking pass confirmed its dead marched toward Quel'Thalas.
Millhaven appears to be the first village on this road fully claimed; settlements beyond are still sick enough to hide the pattern from ordinary travelers.
There was one survivor girl in a cellar.
Neral escorted that girl north with the warning dispatch on horseback.
The party riding into Chapter 4 is only Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel.
They have heard new grain seller once, but do not yet know grain is what carries it.
factsNotYetEarned: Recognition of the trader as anything but a helpful local.

normalizations: Normalized beatType and scenePhase from the markdown beat headings.
Added scene-level arcPressure where the stance test or forced choice is clearly active.
Compressed several sensoryAnchors from sentence-like phrasing into reusable keyword clusters.
Retargeted beat 5 valueShift to inherit beat 4's actual end-state instead of an older draft label.
warnings: Scene 1 beats 1-4 are all scene beats, and the chapter does not reach its first sequel until beat 8. The opening escalation is strong, but the chapter stays scene-heavy for a long stretch.
Beats 1-4 all shift darker in the same direction, and beats 6-8 do it again after the witness turn. The attrition suits the material, but it makes the chapter's pressure unusually relentless.

The current outline supersedes an older investigation-arc summary that still describes a survivor family outside the contamination radius. Kept the cellar-survivor / Neral-dispatch version as operative outline authority for this run.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter2: the same mounted party rides off the rise and into Millhaven proper with no time skip, Jaina's fresh diagnosis still hanging over the group while Sylvanas shifts from distant approach to hands-on village read.

craftResearch.findings: Use interrupted everyday detail, oppressive silence, and delayed explanation so the village feels recently lived in rather than generically spooky.
craftResearch.source: Writing Tips Oasis, Writer's Digest, Talk for Writing suspense guidance via web research

craftResearch.findings: Long horror runs need at least a compressed reaction-review-decision beat or the pressure turns numbing instead of sharp.
craftResearch.source: Dabble, Indie Author Magazine, Not MFA scene-sequel craft guidance via web research

craftResearch.findings: Witness testimony lands best in fragments, sensory flashes, and partial order; extract usable facts without cleaning the memory into tidy exposition.
craftResearch.source: Writers Helping Writers, Novlr, First Draft Pro trauma-writing guidance via web research

craftResearch.findings: Close on an unresolved decision, new question, or deliberate hard cut rather than complete resolution so momentum survives the chapter break.
craftResearch.source: Story Coach, What We Writing, Book Career In A Year chapter-ending guidance via web research

pacing.notes: The chapter opens and stays hot for seven straight scene beats before its first real sequel. The pressure suits the material, but it needed the split-state cleanup at beat 8 and a clear decision beat at 11 to keep the late chapter from blurring into attrition.
details: Beat 1 inherits chapter2 momentum cleanly and does not waste the village entry on recap.
Beats 1 through 4 escalate by distinct discoveries: silence, absence, saturation, then missing dead.
Beats 5 through 7 keep the pressure rising, but the chapter is still scene-heavy enough to risk reader numbness without a real reaction turn.
Beat 8 now functions as a compressed sequel instead of a premature completed split, which restores breathing room without cooling the urgency.
Beat 11 gives the chapter its final emotional reset and hands chapter4 a live forward push instead of a closed investigation unit.

structure.notes: The chapter escalates from entry, to witness, to proof, then cashes the proof into dispatch and renewed pursuit. No reorder was necessary; the main structural fix was clarifying when the northbound split actually becomes real.
details: The opening drops directly into the village sweep, which is the strongest available use of the chapter2 hook.
The middle turn lands around beats 5 through 7, where Millhaven stops being a dead mystery and becomes a border warning with strategic consequences.
Scene 2 is the chapter's true hinge: testimony, then verification, then changed stakes.
Scene 3 works as aftermath plus fresh thread rather than a second climax, which is the right job for the grain-seller lane.
The closing decision preserves unresolved source questions while still completing this chapter's immediate objective.

character.notes: Sylvanas remains the active filter and decision-maker throughout, and the chapter earns its promised shift in how seriously she takes Jaina's read. Supporting characters stay functionally distinct enough to track, with Cyndia and Neral carrying the clearest load-bearing differentiation.
details: Every beat stays filterable through Sylvanas's priorities: threat, terrain, testimony, and consequence.
Sylvanas makes or drives the operative choice in every scene, so the chapter never slips into passive observation.
Jaina stays distinct from the rangers by separating loud magical contamination from meaningful signal rather than by simply naming danger again.
Cyndia's ground competence and Neral's dispatch suitability need to stay distinct from generic escort presence, which the memory annotations now support.
The arc block promises that Jaina's read becomes part of the map, and beats 3, 6, 7, and 11 collectively earn exactly that shift without softening the relationship into comfort.

transitions.notes: All 11 beat-level transitionIntent entries were verified and retyped into explicit causal-pull language. One split-state handoff between beats 8 and 9 was tightened, and the chapter bridge now locks the chapter2-to-chapter3 seam as a same-moment carry.
details: Beat 1 now hands directly into beat 2 by action continuation rather than generic connective phrasing.
Beat 4 correctly plants the need for witness or cleaner proof, and beat 5 pays that need off immediately with the cellar survivor.
Beat 6 now plants a clear confirmation question that beat 7 answers with ground evidence.
Beat 8 no longer claims the split is complete before beat 9 still shows Neral and the survivor on-page.
Beats 10 and 11 now mark deliberate hard cuts so the trader oddity stays faint and chapter4 still lands cleanly after a time jump.

continuity.notes: The chapter now distinguishes new material from ongoing threads cleanly: 3 new beats, 0 callbacks, 8 evolutions. One state contradiction and two anti-reintroduction risks were fixed, and the writer's reading list now includes the Millhaven, vector, escort, and relationship files this chapter actually leans on.
details: Beat 1 is marked evolution, not new, because chapter2 already established Millhaven and the mounted approach.
Beat 3 now treats Jaina's contamination-reading ability as established expertise instead of a fresh surprise.
Beat 6 advances the living-contamination-vector thread while keeping the grain clue weak and unresolved.
Beat 8 cashes out Neral's strongest-rider setup from chapter2 instead of pretending that usefulness begins here.
No memory index contradiction remains once the dispatch timing is clarified and the chapter bridge locks the seam from chapter2.

reshapeAudit: Retyped every transitionIntent into explicit causal-pull language so downstream agents can see how each beat hands off to the next.
Shifted beat 8 from completed separation to split-imminent, preserving beat 9's on-page Neral and survivor state.
Marked beats 1 and 3 as evolutions to prevent Millhaven and Jaina's read from being reintroduced as if chapter2 never happened.
Added targeted verifierNotes where the writer could otherwise overplay the grain clue or over-sell the trader oddity.
Added a direct-carry chapterBridge and requiredMemory list so chapter3 reads as continuation rather than soft restart.

modifications.description: Retyped all 11 beat transitions into explicit causal-pull format and clarified the two hard cuts at the end of the chapter.
Changed beat 8 from a fully completed split to a split-imminent dispatch so beat 9 can still include Neral and the survivor without contradiction.
Annotated every beat with continuityStatus and added memoryRef, verifierNotes, and requiredMemory where prior chapters materially govern the beat.
Added a same-moment chapterBridge so chapter3 opens as direct continuation of chapter2's final approach instead of as a soft restart.
